sections.paragraph: (“sporting,” as if it were part. fut. from the root צָחַק, to which etymology allusion is made, Gen 17:17, 19 Gen 17:19 18:12 , seq.; 21:6 26:8 ), pr.n. Isaac (LXX. Ἰσαάκ), a patriarch, the son of Abraham and Sarah, Gen 21:28. In the poetical books it is sometimes written יִשְׂחָק (Syr. ܐܝܺܣܚܩܳ, Arab. إِسْحَق) Psa 105:9 Jer 33:26 Amo 7:9, 16 and in Am. loc. cit. poetically used for the nation of Israel, i.q. יִשְׂרָאֵל.
